sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INTEGER, created_time TIMESTAMP, event_time TIMESTAMP, event_desc VARCHAR(255));

INSERT INTO test_data VALUES (1, TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 12:00:00', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 13:00:00', 'Event A'), (2, TIMESTAMP '2023-01-02 15:00:00', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 17:00:00', 'Event B'), (3, NULL, TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 18:00:00', NULL);

SELECT id, age(created_time + INTERVAL 2 HOUR) AS age_with_interval, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data t2 WHERE t2.event_time > created_time) AS future_events_count FROM test_data WHERE created_time IS NOT NULL;

WITH ranked_events AS (SELECT id, event_desc, event_time, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY event_desc ORDER BY event_time DESC) AS rn FROM test_data WHERE event_time IS NOT NULL) SELECT id, age(event_time + INTERVAL (rn * 1 HOUR)) AS delayed_age FROM ranked_events WHERE rn = 1;
SELECT event_desc, COUNT(*) AS event_count FROM test_data GROUP BY event_desc HAVING age(MAX(created_time)) > INTERVAL '1 DAY';

SELECT t1.id, t2.event_desc, age(t1.created_time) + INTERVAL 1 HOUR AS adjusted_age FROM test_data t1 JOIN test_data t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t1.created_time IS NOT NULL AND t2.event_time IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INTEGER, time_data TIME, foreign_id INTEGER REFERENCES test_data(id));
INSERT INTO another_table VALUES (1, TIME '01:15:00', 1), (2, TIME '02:30:00', NULL), (3, TIME '03:45:00', 3);
SELECT a.id, a.time_data + INTERVAL 1 HOUR AS time_plus_hour, age(t.event_time) AS event_age FROM another_table a LEFT JOIN test_data t ON a.foreign_id = t.id WHERE a.time_data IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (age(created_time) + INTERVAL 2 HOUR) AS future_adjusted_age FROM test_data WHERE created_time IS NOT NULL ORDER BY future_adjusted_age DESC;
